title: Volt Typhoon Analysis
text: This website presents an overview of Volt Typhoon activity, mapped to MITRE ATT&CK tactics, detection sources, and mitigations.

title: Volt Typhoon Analysis
text:
Detection Sources
This chart shows the ATT&CK detection data components most frequently associated with techniques attributed to Volt Typhoon. Each bar represents the number of ATT&CK detection references that rely on a specific telemetry source, such as process creation events, command execution logs, network traffic content, or user authentication records. Higher values indicate that a particular data source is relevant to detecting a larger portion of Volt Typhoon’s documented techniques.  So what? - Volt Typhoon frequently uses legitimate administrative tools and valid credentials, making behavior-based monitoring more valuable than traditional antivirus signatures. - The telemetry most useful for detecting Volt Typhoon is generated by normal user and system activity—process creation, command execution, authentication events, and network communications. - Effective defense defense against Volt Typhoon depends less on signature-based malware detection and more on maintaining comprehensive visibility into endpoint, authentication, and network behavior.
